 {"id":"ft-akx00.1","title":"Maintain closure ledger for placeholder-remediation program","description":"# Purpose
Maintain the closure ledger for this remediation program so the audit remains complete and falsifiable.

This bead owns the suspect matrix, scope decisions, and final proof that every audited placeholder ended in a deliberate outcome. It exists to prevent the classic failure mode where implementation starts but the inventory stops being tracked.

# Deliverables
- A suspect-by-suspect ledger mapping each audited item to a child bead and final disposition.
- Explicit documentation for any item that gets reclassified as intentional or out-of-scope.
- Final cross-check before the parent epic closes.","design":"Treat the original audit as an immutable input set. For each suspect, record:
- exact file/function/branch,
- why it was considered placeholder behavior,
- which remediation bead owns it,
- what proof is required to declare it closed.

If a task discovers that the optimal fix is deletion or API collapse, record that explicitly here so future readers know it was a principled simplification rather than an omission.","acceptance_criteria":"- The ledger names every verified suspect from the 2026-04-02 audit.
- Each suspect points to exactly one owning child bead or an explicit documented reclassification.
- The final comment on this bead reports any deltas between the initial inventory and the executed work.
- The ledger records not only ownership but also the verification surface for each audited placeholder: unit tests, E2E/integration coverage, and artifact expectations.","notes":"This is a program-control task, not a code task. It should stay open until the end of the entire remediation campaign.

Keep this ledger synchronized with the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8` so future implementers can see both who owns a placeholder and how correctness will be proven.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.213472Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:52.532662Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.213472Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2741,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Initial ledger categories:
- Control plane/client: NTM stub response, client workspace/unilateral handling, client spawn/alt-screen, robot SDK transport.
- Operator support: chrono stub, env-bootstrap/ringlog stubs, scheduled backup compression.
- Search/connectors: transparency verification, certification probe, reranker, model2vec, fastembed sentinel behavior.
- Core runtime/UX: NullPaneScrollbackAccess, recording encodings, FTUI placeholder views, stale PaneCapabilities stub comment.
- Rendering/backends: Wayland seat+keyboard, WGL/macOS/EGL resize, bitmap/WebGPU readback, terminfo raw/crop images, background sizing, custom glyph placeholders.
- Mux/SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle, detach semantics, SFTP metadata, SSH Match Exec.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55Z"},{"id":2772,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Inventory-to-bead ledger for the 2026-04-02 placeholder audit:

Control plane / client / SDK
- `build_ntm_stub_response` fake `"status": "stub"` payload -> `ft-akx00.2.1`
- `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO + unilateral-message FIXME -> `ft-akx00.2.2`
- `ClientDomain::spawn_pane` unimplemented + `ClientPane::is_alt_screen_active()` hardcoded false -> `ft-akx00.2.3`
- generated Rust SDK transport `unimplemented!("transport not wired")` -> `ft-akx00.2.4`

Operator support / persistence utilities
- `chrono_stub_now()` -> `ft-akx00.3.1`
- `env-bootstrap` / `ringlog` stub + no-op logger -> `ft-akx00.3.2`
- scheduled backup compression placeholder -> `ft-akx00.3.3`

Connector / search
- connector transparency verification stub -> `ft-akx00.4.1`
- certification integration probe stub -> `ft-akx00.4.2`
- `CrossEncoderReranker` identity/no-op stub -> `ft-akx00.4.3`
- `Model2VecEmbedder` stub + FastEmbed sentinel constructor -> `ft-akx00.4.4`

Core runtime / FTUI / policy
- `NullPaneScrollbackAccess` placeholder -> `ft-akx00.5.1`
- `DeltaEncoding::Diff` / `Repeat` placeholders -> `ft-akx00.5.2`
- FTUI placeholder view bodies -> `ft-akx00.5.3`
- stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation in `policy.rs` -> `ft-akx00.5.4`

Rendering / windowing
- Wayland seat + keyboard TODO/unimplemented paths -> `ft-akx00.6.1`
- WGL/macOS/EGL resize + framebuffer gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.2`
- bitmap/WebGPU texture readback gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.3`
- terminfo raw/cropped image rendering gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.4`
- GUI background sizing + custom glyph placeholder rendering -> `ft-akx00.6.5`

Mux / SSH
- tmux PTY `try_wait()` + kill placeholders -> `ft-akx00.7.1`
- detach unimplemented across domains -> `ft-akx00.7.2`
- SFTP metadata mutation placeholder -> `ft-akx00.7.3`
- SSH `Match Exec` omission -> `ft-akx00.7.4`

Deliberate exclusions from this epic unless later reclassified by evidence:
- test-only fake panes and other `#[cfg(test)]` doubles
- intentional null-object helpers like `NoopCaptureSink`
- unsupported-platform no-op shims used only to represent platform absence
- vendored/upstream TODOs outside first-party source","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:33:17Z"},{"id":2798,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verification-matrix reminder:
- Shared contract bead: `ft-akx00.8`
- Every concrete child under `ft-akx00` now requires both comprehensive unit tests and a dedicated or explicitly assigned E2E/integration scenario with structured logs and artifacts.
- Completion evidence for future implementation beads should cite the exact proposed script name (or renamed final equivalent), exact commands, artifact root, and whether heavy validation ran via `rch`.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.2","title":"[EPIC] Complete machine-control, client-domain, and SDK placeholder surfaces","description":"Own all audited placeholders that directly affect machine-control surfaces, client-domain behavior, and SDK usability.

This track covers CLI/API envelopes that currently fake success, client-domain branches that silently drop important state, and generated SDK code that advertises an implementation it does not yet have.","design":"Prioritize user-visible truthfulness:
- remove fake stub envelopes first,
- ensure client state propagation is real before layering more commands on top,
- wire SDK transport only after the underlying behavior is trustworthy.","acceptance_criteria":"- No audited control-plane or client-domain placeholder remains under this track.
- Client-side state transitions and pane lifecycle features have regression tests.
- SDK surfaces fail or succeed for real reasons rather than `unimplemented!()` or fake status payloads.
- Every concrete child bead under this epic closes with comprehensive unit tests plus a dedicated or explicitly assigned E2E/integration scenario, all executed under the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Primary files: `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`, `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, `frankenterm/client/src/domain.rs`, `frankenterm/client/src/pane/clientpane.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs`.

## Track Verification Emphasis
- CLI/robot/client/SDK contract tests plus end-to-end command or transport scripts with request-id, domain-id, pane-id, and error-code logging.
- Heavy validation runs in this track must capture structured logs, exact command transcripts, and artifact paths.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.699527Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:29.173500Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["client","control-plane","e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","sdk","stub-audit","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.2","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.699527Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2742,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"This track carries the highest trust risk because these surfaces are exactly where humans and agents form beliefs about what the system can do. Silent placeholders here create cascading bad decisions elsewhere in the stack.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.2.1","title":"Replace build_ntm_stub_response with real NTM status and error plumbing","description":"Replace the audited placeholder at `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs::build_ntm_stub_response`, which currently emits a machine-readable payload with `"status": "stub"` instead of describing real behavior.

The task is to either implement real NTM command/status plumbing or replace the fake-success envelope with an explicit unsupported/unavailable contract that callers can rely on.","design":"1. Trace all code paths that call `build_ntm_stub_response` and classify them as real success, unavailable backend, unsupported mode, or internal failure.
2. Define one canonical response schema for those cases instead of ad hoc status strings.
3. Ensure the schema is shared across human CLI output and machine-readable JSON/TOON where relevant.
4. Remove any remaining literal `stub` success signal from the command path.","acceptance_criteria":"- No production NTM path returns `"status": "stub"`.
- Success cases surface real state; unsupported/unavailable cases surface explicit structured errors.
- Regression tests cover success, unsupported, and unavailable-backend branches.
- Help/docs/comments no longer imply the command is implemented when it is not.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Primary location: `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` around the NTM command routing and response helpers.

  - command name, backend selection, response branch, error code, duration, artifact dir
  - payload snapshots and reproduction commands","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:46Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-akx00.2.3","title":"Complete ClientDomain pane lifecycle: spawn_pane and authoritative alt-screen state","description":"Replace two audited client-domain placeholders:
- `ClientDomain::spawn_pane` currently bails with `spawn_pane not implemented for ClientDomain`.
- `ClientPane::is_alt_screen_active()` currently hardcodes `false` with a FIXME saying the state should come from the remote.

The goal is real remote pane lifecycle support and real safety-relevant screen-state truth.","design":"1. Extend or reuse the remote protocol/state cache so pane metadata includes authoritative alt-screen state.
2. Implement remote `spawn_pane` semantics in a way that integrates with existing domain/session bookkeeping instead of bypassing it.
3. Ensure alt-screen truth flows through the same state pipeline used by policy and input safety gates.
4. Add tests for spawn success/failure and alt-screen transitions.","acceptance_criteria":"- `ClientDomain::spawn_pane` performs real work or emits a deliberately explicit unsupported error backed by feature policy, not a raw placeholder bail.

- Safety-sensitive send/input paths can rely on the resulting alt-screen data.
- Tests cover remote spawn lifecycle and alt-screen state updates.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.

 {"id":"ft-akx00.2.4","title":"Wire generated Rust robot SDK transport into a real control-plane backend","description":"The generated Rust SDK client in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs` still bottoms out in `unimplemented!("transport not wired")`.

This task owns turning the generated surface into a genuinely usable SDK by wiring it to the chosen transport/backend layer and validating the contract end to end.","design":"1. Decide whether the SDK should speak directly to robot IPC, a local HTTP/MCP bridge, or another canonical transport.
2. Implement a transport adapter that preserves typed request/response envelopes and error codes.
3. Ensure generated code does not imply a capability that only exists in handwritten paths.
4. Add at least one end-to-end contract test that uses the generated client against a real backend shim.","acceptance_criteria":"- The generated Rust SDK no longer contains `unimplemented!("transport not wired")` in a shipping path.
- At least one supported transport backend is documented and tested.
- Error mapping preserves robot-mode semantics rather than collapsing everything into generic failures.
- Users of the generated client can discover unsupported operations explicitly.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.

Investigating the generated Rust robot SDK path with the current working hypothesis that the real backend should be the existing watcher IPC RPC surface (`frankenterm_core::ipc::IpcClient::call_rpc`) rather than a new transport. Current goals: eliminate the rendered `unimplemented!("transport not wired")`, preserve `robot.*` error semantics through typed client helpers, and add an end-to-end transport test/artifact path under the `ft-akx00.8` contract.","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:32:10Z"},{"id":2810,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.2.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented the Rust robot SDK transport against the existing watcher IPC RPC backend instead of the rendered unimplemented stub. The generated Rust client now wraps a compiled `RustSdkTransport` helper, exposes explicit supported-command discovery, maps contract commands to current IPC/CLI args (`send-text` -> `send`), and preserves robot envelope/error semantics through typed decoding. Also refreshed the core endpoint specs so request/response fields match the current robot surfaces more closely, and added a dedicated integration suite `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/robot_sdk_transport_integration.rs` that exercises a real IPC server + RPC handler roundtrip.

Verification artifacts: `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.2.4/scoped-verification/20260403T034437Z`
- `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib rust_sdk_transport_ -- --nocapture` via `rch` passed on `vmi1227854`
- `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test robot_sdk_transport_integration -- --nocapture` via `rch` passed on `vmi1227854`
